tools.name: safari_navigate
tools.description: Navigate to a URL in Safari. Waits for page to fully load.

tools.name: safari_go_back
tools.description: Go back in browser history

tools.name: safari_go_forward
tools.description: Go forward in browser history

tools.name: safari_reload
tools.description: Reload the current page

tools.name: safari_read_page
tools.description: Read page text content (title, URL, body text). Use for reading article text or page content. For interacting with elements, prefer safari_snapshot (gives ref IDs). Use selector to read specific element. Use maxLength to limit output.

selector.description: CSS selector to read specific element

tools.name: safari_get_source
tools.description: Get HTML source of current page

tools.name: safari_snapshot
tools.description: PREFERRED way to see page state. Returns accessibility tree with ref IDs for every interactive element. Use refs with click/fill/type instead of CSS selectors. Workflow: snapshot → see refs → click({ref:'0_5'}). PREFER THIS over safari_screenshot (cheaper, structured text vs heavy image) and over safari_read_page (includes interactive refs). Use safari_screenshot only when you need to see visual layout/styling.

selector.description: CSS selector for subtree (default: full page)

tools.name: safari_navigate_and_read
tools.description: Navigate to a URL and return the page content in one step — saves 1 full round-trip vs navigate+read_page. Use instead of safari_navigate + safari_read_page.

tools.name: safari_click
tools.description: Click element. Use ref (from snapshot), selector, text, or x/y. Works on React/Airtable/virtual DOM apps via full PointerEvent+MouseEvent sequence + React Fiber fallback. Pure JS — never touches user's mouse. When using ref, always take a FRESH safari_snapshot first — refs expire after each new snapshot.

text.description: Visible text to find and click

tools.name: safari_click_and_read
tools.description: Click an element then return the updated page — saves 1 full round-trip vs separate click+read_page. Handles both React Router navigation and full page loads.

text.description: Visible text of the element to click

wait.description: Ms to wait after click (default: auto-detect navigation)

tools.name: safari_double_click
tools.description: Double-click an element by CSS selector or x/y coordinates (e.g. to select a word in text)

tools.name: safari_right_click
tools.description: Right-click (context menu) an element by CSS selector or x/y coordinates

tools.name: safari_native_click
tools.description: OS-level mouse click via macOS CGEvent — produces isTrusted: true events that pass WAF/bot detection (G2, Cloudflare, etc.). Use when regular safari_click fails with 405/403 errors or form submissions are blocked. Trade-off: physically moves the mouse cursor and requires Safari window to be visible. Use ref (from snapshot), selector, text, or x/y. When using ref, always take a FRESH safari_snapshot first.

text.description: Visible text to find and click

tools.name: safari_fill
tools.description: Fill/replace value in an input, textarea, select, OR contenteditable (rich text). Handles React controlled inputs, ProseMirror, Draft.js, and Google Closure editors automatically. Use for SETTING a value (replaces existing). For code editors (Monaco/CodeMirror/Ace), use safari_replace_editor instead. For character-by-character typing in search boxes, use safari_type_text. IMPORTANT: When using ref, always take a FRESH safari_snapshot first — refs expire after each new snapshot (prefix changes: 5_xx → 6_xx).

tools.name: safari_clear_field
tools.description: Clear an input field

tools.name: safari_select_option
tools.description: Select an option in a native <select> dropdown. Sets .value and dispatches change event. For custom dropdowns (React/LinkedIn), use safari_click on the dropdown trigger, then safari_click on the option instead.

tools.name: safari_fill_form
tools.description: Fill multiple form fields at once

tools.name: safari_press_key
tools.description: Press a keyboard key (enter, tab, escape, arrows, etc). Supports modifiers (cmd, shift, alt, ctrl).

key.description: Key name: enter, tab, escape, space, delete, up, down, left, right, or a single character

modifiers.description: Modifier keys: cmd, shift, alt, ctrl

tools.name: safari_type_text
tools.description: Type text character-by-character with realistic key events. Best for: search boxes (triggers autocomplete), chat inputs, and fields that react to each keystroke. For rich text editors (Medium, HackerNoon, LinkedIn), use safari_fill instead — it uses framework-native APIs. For code editors (Monaco/CodeMirror), use safari_replace_editor. When using ref, always take a FRESH safari_snapshot first — refs expire after each new snapshot.

tools.name: safari_replace_editor
tools.description: Replace ALL content in a code editor (Monaco, CodeMirror, Ace, ProseMirror). Use ONLY for code editors — Airtable automations, GitHub gists, CodePen, n8n code nodes, etc. NOT for rich text editors like Medium/LinkedIn (use safari_fill for those). Detects ProseMirror/Draft.js/CodeMirror/Monaco/Ace and uses their native API.

text.description: The complete code/text to put in the editor

tools.name: safari_screenshot
tools.description: Take a visual screenshot (base64 JPEG). EXPENSIVE — use safari_snapshot instead for most tasks. Only use screenshot when you need to verify visual layout, styling, images, or colors that snapshot can't show.

tools.name: safari_screenshot_element
tools.description: Take a screenshot of a specific element (by CSS selector). Returns base64 PNG image.

selector.description: CSS selector of the element to capture

tools.name: safari_scroll
tools.description: Scroll the page up or down by a specified amount

tools.name: safari_scroll_to
tools.description: Scroll to a specific position on the page

tools.name: safari_list_tabs
tools.description: List all open tabs in Safari with their titles and URLs

tools.name: safari_new_tab
tools.description: Open a new tab, optionally with a URL

url.description: URL to open (empty for blank tab)

tools.name: safari_close_tab
tools.description: Close the current tab

tools.name: safari_switch_tab
tools.description: Switch to a specific tab by index (use safari_list_tabs to see indices). All subsequent commands (click, fill, evaluate, screenshot, scroll) will target this tab. If commands seem to run on the wrong tab, call switch_tab again to re-anchor.

tools.name: safari_wait_for
tools.description: Wait for an element or text to appear on the page

tools.name: safari_wait_for_new_tab
tools.description: Wait for a new tab to appear (e.g. after OAuth login click opens popup). Automatically switches to the new tab.

urlContains.description: Only match new tabs whose URL contains this string

tools.name: safari_evaluate
tools.description: Execute JavaScript in the current page. Automatically falls back to AppleScript when CSP blocks execution (e.g. Google Search Console, LinkedIn). For reading data, prefer safari_read_page or safari_snapshot. For interactions, prefer safari_click/fill with refs.

tools.name: safari_get_element
tools.description: Get detailed info about an element (tag, text, rect, attributes, visibility)

tools.name: safari_query_all
tools.description: Find all elements matching a CSS selector (returns tag, text, href, value)

tools.name: safari_hover
tools.description: Hover over element. Use ref, selector, or x/y

tools.name: safari_handle_dialog
tools.description: Set up handler for the next alert/confirm/prompt dialog

text.description: Text to enter for prompt dialogs

tools.name: safari_resize
tools.description: Resize the Safari window

tools.name: safari_drag
tools.description: Drag an element to another element or position. Use CSS selectors or x/y coordinates.

sourceSelector.description: CSS selector of element to drag

tools.name: safari_upload_file
tools.description: Upload a file to a <input type='file'> element via JavaScript DataTransfer — NO file dialog, NO UI interaction. IMPORTANT: Do NOT click the file input before calling this tool — just provide the selector and file path. If a file dialog is already open, this tool will close it first. NOTE: 'verified 0 files' may appear even on success if the site uses a custom upload handler — check visually with safari_snapshot.

selector.description: CSS selector of the file input

filePath.description: Absolute path to the file to upload

tools.name: safari_paste_image
tools.description: Paste an image from a local file into the focused element via JS DataTransfer (no clipboard, no focus steal). Works on Medium, dev.to, HackerNoon, TOI, etc.

filePath.description: Absolute path to the image file (PNG, JPG, WebP)

tools.name: safari_emulate
tools.description: Emulate a mobile device by resizing window and setting user agent. Devices: iphone-14, iphone-14-pro-max, ipad, ipad-pro, pixel-7, galaxy-s24. Or use custom width/height.

device.description: Device name: iphone-14, ipad, pixel-7, galaxy-s24, etc.

tools.name: safari_reset_emulation
tools.description: Reset device emulation back to desktop mode

tools.name: safari_get_cookies
tools.description: Get cookies for the current page

tools.name: safari_local_storage
tools.description: Get localStorage data for the current page

key.description: Specific key to get (omit for all)

tools.name: safari_network
tools.description: Quick network overview via Performance API (no setup needed). Shows URLs and timing for resources loaded by the page. For detailed request/response info (headers, status codes, POST bodies), use safari_start_network_capture + safari_network_details instead.

tools.name: safari_run_script
tools.description: Batch multiple Safari actions in ONE call. Steps: [{action, args}]. Actions match other safari_* tool names without prefix (e.g. 'navigate', 'click', 'fill', 'evaluate', 'readPage').

steps.description: Array of steps to execute sequentially

tools.name: safari_start_console
tools.description: Start capturing console messages (log, warn, error, info). Call once per page.

tools.name: safari_get_console
tools.description: Get captured console messages (must call safari_start_console first)

tools.name: safari_clear_console
tools.description: Clear all captured console messages

tools.name: safari_save_pdf
tools.description: Save the current page as a PDF file. Uses screencapture + PDF rendering (no Safari UI interaction needed).

path.description: Absolute file path to save the PDF (e.g. /Users/am/Downloads/page.pdf)

tools.name: safari_accessibility_snapshot
tools.description: Get the accessibility tree of the page (roles, ARIA labels, focusable elements, form states). Essential for a11y auditing.

selector.description: CSS selector for subtree (default: full page)

tools.name: safari_set_cookie
tools.description: Set a cookie on the current page

tools.name: safari_delete_cookies
tools.description: Delete a specific cookie or all cookies for the current page

tools.name: safari_session_storage
tools.description: Get sessionStorage data for the current page

tools.name: safari_set_session_storage
tools.description: Set a value in sessionStorage

tools.name: safari_set_local_storage
tools.description: Set a value in localStorage

tools.name: safari_delete_local_storage
tools.description: Delete a localStorage key, or clear all localStorage (omit key to clear all)

key.description: Key to delete (omit to clear ALL)

tools.name: safari_delete_session_storage
tools.description: Delete a sessionStorage key, or clear all sessionStorage (omit key to clear all)

key.description: Key to delete (omit to clear ALL)

tools.name: safari_export_storage
tools.description: Export all storage state (cookies + localStorage + sessionStorage) as JSON — useful for saving and restoring login sessions

tools.name: safari_import_storage
tools.description: Import storage state from JSON (as exported by safari_export_storage) — restores cookies, localStorage, sessionStorage

tools.name: safari_clipboard_read
tools.description: Read the current clipboard content (text)

tools.name: safari_clipboard_write
tools.description: Write text to the system clipboard

tools.name: safari_mock_route
tools.description: Intercept network requests matching a URL pattern and return a mock response. Works with both fetch and XHR. Useful for testing API error states, offline behavior, or replacing API responses.

urlPattern.description: URL substring or regex pattern to match (e.g. '/api/users' or 'example\.com')

tools.name: safari_clear_mocks
tools.description: Remove all network route mocks (restore real network behavior)

tools.name: safari_wait
tools.description: Wait for a fixed time in milliseconds. Use only when you need a brief pause between actions. PREFER safari_wait_for (waits for element/text to appear) — it's smarter and doesn't waste time.

tools.name: safari_start_network_capture
tools.description: Start capturing detailed network requests (fetch + XHR) with headers, status, timing. Call once per page. Intercepts fetch/XHR — captures requests AFTER this call only. For quick overview of already-loaded resources, use safari_network instead.

tools.name: safari_network_details
tools.description: Get captured network requests with full details (must call safari_start_network_capture first)

tools.name: safari_clear_network
tools.description: Clear all captured network requests

tools.name: safari_performance_metrics
tools.description: Get detailed performance metrics: navigation timing, Web Vitals (FCP, LCP, CLS), resource breakdown, memory usage

tools.name: safari_throttle_network
tools.description: Simulate slow network conditions. Profiles: slow-3g, fast-3g, 4g, offline. Or custom latency/speed. Call with no args to reset.

tools.name: safari_console_filter
tools.description: Get console messages filtered by level (must call safari_start_console first)

tools.name: safari_extract_tables
tools.description: Extract HTML tables as structured JSON (headers + rows). Perfect for scraping data tables.

tools.name: safari_extract_meta
tools.description: Extract all meta tags: title, description, canonical, OG tags, Twitter cards, JSON-LD, alternate languages, RSS feeds

tools.name: safari_extract_images
tools.description: Extract all images with src, alt, dimensions, loading strategy, viewport visibility

tools.name: safari_extract_links
tools.description: Extract all links with href, text, rel, target, external/nofollow detection

filter.description: Filter by URL or text substring

tools.name: safari_override_geolocation
tools.description: Override the browser's geolocation API to return custom coordinates

tools.name: safari_get_computed_style
tools.description: Get computed CSS styles for an element. Optionally filter specific properties.

tools.name: safari_list_indexed_dbs
tools.description: List all IndexedDB databases on the current page

tools.name: safari_get_indexed_db
tools.description: Read records from an IndexedDB database store

tools.name: safari_css_coverage
tools.description: Analyze CSS coverage: find unused CSS rules across all stylesheets. Shows coverage percentage per stylesheet.

tools.name: safari_detect_forms
tools.description: Auto-detect all forms on the page with their fields, types, selectors, and submit buttons. Great for automated form filling.

tools.name: safari_scroll_to_element
tools.description: Scroll to element by CSS selector OR text. For virtual DOM (Airtable) use text — scrolls down until text appears in DOM.

text.description: Text to find — scrolls down until it appears (for virtual DOM/lazy loading)

timeout.description: Max time to scroll in ms (default: 10000)

tools.name: safari_click_and_wait
tools.description: Click an element AND wait for the result (page load or element). Use instead of click + wait_for separately.

waitFor.description: CSS selector to wait for after click

tools.name: safari_fill_and_submit
tools.description: Fill a form AND submit it in one operation. Finds submit button automatically if not specified.

tools.name: safari_analyze_page
tools.description: Full page analysis in ONE call: title, URL, meta tags, OG, headings, link stats, image stats, forms, and text preview. Perfect for SEO/audit.
